2012-07-01.title: A Telling Language Lesson in Hong Kong
2012-07-01.abstract: A new chief executive took the oath of office in Hong Kong on Sunday morning, and then, somewhat astoundingly, he made his inaugural speech in Mandarin Chinese, the language of the mainland. In spurning Cantonese, spoken by virtually all of Hong Kong, Leung Chun-ying might as well have knelt on stage in front of President Hu Jintao of China in a full kowtow to Beijing.

2012-07-01.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-07-01.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-07-01.title: Protesters March as New Hong Kong Leader Is Sworn In
2012-07-01.abstract: The demonstrations reflected concerns that the new chief executive, Leung Chun-ying, may roll back some of the city’s cherished civil liberties.

2012-07-01.title: At Least 15 Die in Kenya Church Attacks
2012-07-01.abstract: Masked gunmen hurled grenades into two churches in northern Kenya and sprayed gunfire at worshipers fleeing one of them.

2012-07-01.title: Newly Elected Mexican Leader Pledges Transparency
2012-07-01.abstract: President-elect Enrique Peña Nieto began the transition to power on Monday, declaring that he would begin to name members of his cabinet, and pledging to defeat organized crime and improve the economy.

2012-07-02.title: Russians and Syrians, Allied by History and Related by Marriage
2012-07-02.abstract: An estimated 20,000 Russian women live in Syria, married to Syrian men who studied in the former Soviet Union, an unusual diaspora offering insight into the two countries’ relations.

2012-07-02.title: A Salve to National Pride as Spain Wins Euro Title
2012-07-02.abstract: The success of La Roja, or the Red, as Spain’s team is known, has — at least momentarily — helped overshadow the country’s deepening recession and banking crisis.

2012-07-02.title: In Olympic Infighting, the Koreas Take the Gold
2012-07-02.abstract: North and South Korea will not be marching together at the opening of the London Olympics three weeks hence. The bilateral relationship is in bad shape, and even though there’s a new Kim in charge, the North keeps threatening to turn the South into a sea of fire.

2012-07-02.title: Malana’s Cream Brings Problems and Profits
2012-07-02.abstract: A remote Himachal Pradesh community famed for its hashish draws tourists from around the world.

2012-07-02.title: Crippled, Chaotic Pakistan
2012-07-02.abstract: After 2001, Pakistan had a chance to develop into a more stable country, but that chance has been thwarted.

2012-07-03.title: U.S. and Pakistan Hint at Deal to Reopen NATO Supply Routes
2012-07-03.abstract: American and Pakistani officials, without discussing details, voiced cautious optimism that a deal to reopen the routes into Afghanistan was near after a seven-month stalemate.

2012-07-03.title: Libya Frees Four From International Court’s Team
2012-07-03.abstract: The release ends a monthlong diplomatic tussle over a group that was intended to have immunity.

2012-07-03.title: For Mexico’s President-Elect, a Strategic Journey
2012-07-03.abstract: Enrique Peña Nieto is a consummate political animal, but how he will govern — as a proxy for his party’s old guard, or with a new, more humbled vision — remains a bit of a mystery.

2012-07-03.title: Israel Disbands Panel on Military Service Rules
2012-07-03.abstract: In a rift that could threaten Israel’s unity government, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu and his coalition partner, Shaul Mofaz, disagree on what to do about ultra-Orthodox men who avoid service.

2012-07-03.title: Foreign-Funded Nonprofits in Russia Face New Hurdle
2012-07-03.abstract: Authorities are working to push through a law that would force organizations receiving foreign financing to declare themselves “foreign agents.”

2012-07-04.title: France: Web Administrator Charged in Terror Inquiry
2012-07-04.abstract: The French police filed preliminary charges of planning terrorist acts and financing a terrorism enterprise on Tuesday against the administrator of an extremist Islamic Web site.

2012-07-04.title: Al Jazeera Says Arafat Might Have Been Poisoned
2012-07-04.abstract: The widow of Yasir Arafat, the Palestinian nationalist leader who mysteriously died in 2004, is calling for an inquest after a report by Al Jazeera that said he might have been poisoned.

2012-07-04.title: Iran: U.N. and U.S. Condemn an Anti-Semitic Speech
2012-07-04.abstract: A United Nations official condemned Iran’s vice president for a speech at an anti-drug conference in Iran in which he blamed the Talmud, a central text of Judaism, for promoting drug addiction.

2012-07-04.title: Belarus: Journalist Indicted on Libel Charges
2012-07-04.abstract: A journalist based in western Belarus has been indicted on libel charges after alleging that President Aleksandr G. Lukashenko influenced a court decision to execute two men.

2012-07-04.title: Lebanon Artists Confront Rise in Censorship
2012-07-04.abstract: Four new films have been banned this year — a record for the Media and Theater Department, as the censorship bureau is formally called.

2012-07-05.title: Ukrainian Official Quits to Protest Russian-Language Bill
2012-07-05.abstract: By tendering his resignation, the chairman of Parliament temporarily blocked the contentious measure from reaching the president.

2012-07-05.title: Oil Backed Up, Iranians Put It on Idled Ships
2012-07-05.abstract: As its oil inventory grows because of tough sanctions, Iran has taken to repainting and renaming its tankers, concealing their origins in a bid to find buyers.

2012-07-05.title: Socialists in France Announce New Taxes
2012-07-05.abstract: France’s new Socialist government announced billions of euros in new taxes and tax increases, designed to bring the country’s deficit down in a period of nearly stagnant growth.

2012-07-05.title: Arab Envoys Rebuked for Denying Prize Money to Algerian Writer
2012-07-05.abstract: Arab diplomats in France have been criticized for quietly withdrawing the prize money for a literary award to Boualem Sansal, a famous Algerian novelist, because he visited Israel.

2012-07-05.title: What’s Killing Cambodia’s Children?
2012-07-05.abstract: This is the grim track of a mysterious new illness that has already killed scores of children in Cambodia: First, a severe fever. Then encephalitis, a swelling of the brain. Finally, the lungs go, and the child dies. All this happens within 24 hours.

2012-07-06.title: Anderson Cooper’s Coming Out Rattles China’s Closet
2012-07-06.abstract: In a surprising — or not — example of our interconnected world, the public declaration of a celebrity journalist inspires people a world away, living in much harsher circumstances.

2012-07-06.title: A Factory Grows in Haiti
2012-07-06.abstract: The showcase project for Haiti’s earthquake reconstruction is being built far outside the disaster zone, in a location that could jeopardize the country’s key conservation effort.

2012-07-06.title: Environmental and Labor Concerns in Haiti Reconstruction
2012-07-06.abstract: Two and a half years after the earthquake, the reconstruction effort includes an industrial park that risks benefiting foreign companies more than Haiti itself.

2012-07-06.title: Earthquake Relief Where Haiti Wasn’t Broken
2012-07-06.abstract: As rebuilding lags, the showcase project of reconstruction is an industrial park in an undamaged area, a venture that risks benefiting foreign companies more than Haiti itself.

2012-07-06.title: China: Security Increases at 16 Airports
2012-07-06.abstract: The western region of Xinjiang has increased security measures at all 16 of its airports after a failed hijacking attempt.

2012-07-07.title: After Liberté and Égalité, It’s Autopsie
2012-07-07.abstract: Philippe Charlier, a physician and anthropologist, is known for his forensic research into some of France’s most famous dead.

2012-07-07.title: U.S. Presses Iranian Dissidents to Vacate Camp
2012-07-07.abstract: The administration suggested the group’s conduct will influence a decision on whether to continue its official designation as a terrorist organization.

2012-07-07.title: A Closer Look at One Carrier’s Deployment
2012-07-07.abstract: Weapons use is infrequent. The aircraft carrier John C. Stennis was stationed near Afghanistan twice during last fall and winter. Of the 892 F/A-18 sorties from the ship in the two trips, weapons were discharged on 41.

2012-07-07.title: Before Vote, Old Rivalries Threaten Fresh Start in Libya
2012-07-07.abstract: Many Libyans still hope that the election of a new national congress will offer a more peaceful way to resolve differences.

2012-07-07.title: In Libya, a Divided Nation Prepares to Vote
2012-07-07.abstract: With elections scheduled for Saturday, and a weak interim government, those divisions between Libya’s regions and tribes have been magnified, threatening to tear the nation apart as it struggles to build a unified national identity.

2012-07-08.title: A Young Syrian’s Evolution From a Carefree Tour Guide to a Revolutionary
2012-07-08.abstract: A Bedouin man has struggled with uncertainty over whether joining a revolt was life’s proudest moment or its ruin — or both.

2012-07-08.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-07-08.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-07-08.title: Girl Guides Change Oath to Delete God and the Queen
2012-07-08.abstract: The new oath adopted by Girl Guides Australia removes references to God and the queen, and the word “obedience” has been dropped from the Girl Guide Law. Leaders say the new wording is more inclusive, more relevant to an increasingly multicultural Australia. Not everyone, of course, agrees.

2012-07-08.title: $16 Billion in Civilian Aid Pledged to Afghanistan, With Conditions
2012-07-08.abstract: The donors at an international conference insisted for the first time that the Afghan government resist corruption in order to receive all the money.

2012-07-08.title: In Video of Execution, Reign of Taliban Recalled
2012-07-08.abstract: A graphic video circulating in Kabul recalls the Taliban’s five-year reign in Afghanistan, when people accused of crimes were shot in front of crowds that packed the capital’s stadium.

2012-07-09.title: First a Building Spree, Now the Ax Is Falling
2012-07-09.abstract: In recent years Spain’s autonomous regions built lavish projects, including a mega-movie studio in Valencia, with tax dollars. Now Valencia must explain how it will recoup taxpayers’ money.

2012-07-09.title: Germany and France Celebrate Their Bond
2012-07-09.abstract: Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany and François Hollande, the new French president, met on the 50th anniversary of a handshake meant to end a century of enmity between their countries.

2012-07-09.title: An Experiment That Could Put Women in More Combat Jobs
2012-07-09.abstract: No one seriously doubts whether women can fight, kill or share the risks, pains and sacrifices with men. But no one knows what full-bore service as a female grunt would look like. That is about to change.

2012-07-09.title: Drug Smuggling Suspect Killed by D.E.A. Agents in Honduras
2012-07-09.abstract: The shooting, the second in a month, was the latest demonstration of the growing American involvement in counternarcotics operations in the country.

2012-07-09.title: The Latin Prize, 1942
2012-07-09.abstract: On Tuesday, France will award this year’s Latin Prize to the high school student who scored highest in the nation in the concours general. Seventy years ago that was Richard Fligelman.

2012-07-10.title: The Grandmothers Sang ‘Come On and Dance!’ and a Village Got New Life
2012-07-10.abstract: After the Buranovskiye Babushki became a hit at the Eurovision song contest and on the Internet, improvements came to the village of Buranovo.

2012-07-10.title: U.S. Antagonist in Iraq Takes a Political Gamble
2012-07-10.abstract: Moktada al-Sadr, the former leader of the anti-American Mahdi Army, is trying to navigate his way to national power through nontraditional alliances that risk alienating supporters.

2012-07-10.title: Iran: Nuclear Meeting Set for July 24
2012-07-10.abstract: Deputies of the lead negotiators for Iran and the group of six nations in the Iranian nuclear dispute will meet in Turkey on July 24.

2012-07-10.title: The Hague: Mladic’s Trial Resumes
2012-07-10.abstract: Ratko Mladic, the wartime Bosnian Serb general, returned to court on Monday to face the first witness testifying against him in his war crimes trial at The Hague.

2012-07-10.title: Robert de La Rochefoucauld, Wartime Hero and Spy, Dies at 88
2012-07-10.abstract: Mr. de La Rochefoucauld is best remembered as a celebrated saboteur who fought for the honor of France in World War II as a secret agent with the British.

2012-07-11.title: Judge Dismisses Suit by Londoners Wary of Olympic Air Defenses on Their Roof
2012-07-11.abstract: The ruling endorsed the argument that air defense weapons in a residential area in London were necessary to ward off a terrorist strike.

2012-07-11.title: Medieval French Village Echoes With the Voice of Kennedy’s Camelot
2012-07-11.abstract: A museum to Pierre Salinger, John F. Kennedy’s jovial spokesman, is tucked away in the Provençal village where Mr. Salinger spent the last years of his life.

2012-07-11.title: Flooding Aftermath in Russia
2012-07-11.abstract: An extraordinary rainstorm in southern Russia set off floods that killed at least 104 people over the weekend.

2012-07-11.title: Spreading the Faith Where Faith Itself Is Suspect
2012-07-11.abstract: As the Roman Catholic Church tries to grow in China, it increasingly finds itself in conflict with the authorities.

2012-07-11.title: After Russian Floods, Grief, Rage and Deep Mistrust
2012-07-11.abstract: Officials acknowledged that they had been aware of a threat to Krymsk the night before deadly flooding, but had not taken measures to rouse its sleeping residents.

2012-07-12.title: Tetra Pak, a Fortune Founded on a Clever Idea
2012-07-12.abstract: The mysterious death of an American philanthropist has cast a spotlight on the source of her husband’s vast wealth: the stackable, light and ubiquitous drink box.

2012-07-12.title: Spain’s Leader Plans New Austerity Steps as Miners Clash With Police
2012-07-12.abstract: The measures from Prime Minister Mariano Rajoy, which include a rise in the national sales tax to 21 percent, are designed to reduce Spain’s budget deficit by $80 billion over the next two and a half years.

2012-07-12.title: Britain: Olympics Duty for 3,500 More Troops
2012-07-12.abstract: A major shortfall in the number of private security guards available to protect sporting venues and other sensitive sites has forced the government to draft additional troops.

2012-07-12.title: Attacks on Mexico Papers Underline Peril to Journalists
2012-07-12.abstract: Attacks on the news media in Mexico, in the throes of a drug war, are seen as an effort to control information.

2012-07-12.title: Tehran Abuzz as Book Says Israel Killed 5 Scientists
2012-07-12.abstract: A new book, “Spies Against Armageddon: Inside Israel’s Secret Wars,” contends that the five Iranian nuclear scientists killed in the past five years were assassinated by Israel’s intelligence agency, the Mossad.

2012-07-13.title: In Simply Meeting, Egyptian and Saudi Leaders Open New Era
2012-07-13.abstract: Mohamed Morsi of the Muslim Brotherhood met Thursday with King Abdullah of Saudi Arabia, a signal that the two intended to set aside their profound ideological enmity in favor of pragmatic mutual interests.

2012-07-13.title: Planning to Study in America? Tips for Navigating Outside the Classroom
2012-07-13.abstract: The author, a graduate of a Mumbai high school now at New York University, advises Indian students to be wary of declining a social invitation, as it might not be extended again.

2012-07-13.title: Young North Korean Defectors Struggle in the South
2012-07-13.abstract: Attempts at integration, including education at top universities, have had mixed results, leaving many North Koreans unable to adapt to South Korea’s hard-charging society.

2012-07-13.title: Money, Power, Politics and Corruption: U.S. vs. China
2012-07-13.abstract: We expect our leaders to be honest. But do we also expect them to be poor? In politics, when does influence peddling slip over into outright corruption? Where’s the gray zone? Where, in fact, are the limits — in China, in the United States, or elsewhere?

2012-07-13.title: At Trial Over Racial Slur, a Redefining of ‘Acceptable Language’
2012-07-13.abstract: John Terry and Anton Ferdinand, two professional soccer players, laid out an exchange of paint-peeling profanities that led to a charge of racial abuse.

2012-07-14.title: A Priest Stands Up for the Migrants Who Run Mexico’s Gantlet
2012-07-14.abstract: The Rev. Alejandro Solalinde has raised Mexicans’ awareness of the plight of Central and South American migrants who face extortion, rape, kidnapping and murder en route to the United States.

2012-07-14.title: Syria Moving Parts of Chemical Arsenal, U.S. Says
2012-07-14.abstract: It was uncertain whether the transfer was intended to keep chemical weapons out of rebel hands or was for more sinister purposes, officials and analysts said.

2012-07-14.title: Egypt’s New President Is Being Undercut by State-Run Media
2012-07-14.abstract: State broadcasters and newspapers have quickly allied with the Egypt’s generals over President Mohamed Morsi, making it clear who still holds the real power over Egypt’s bureaucracy.

2012-07-14.title: Iran’s Aging Airliner Fleet Seen as Faltering Under U.S. Sanctions
2012-07-14.abstract: Iranians say that sanctions have barred the country from updating its jetliners and left them to patch up planes that are too old to fly.

2012-07-14.title: U.S. Charges Men in Plot to Violate Iran Embargo
2012-07-14.abstract: The men, one from China and one from Iran, are accused of trying to smuggle in materials for enriching uranium into Iran.

2012-07-15.title: ‘Ruin Porn’ — the Aftermath of the Beijing Olympics
2012-07-15.abstract: The photographic genre of ruin porn — those grim pictures of mothballed auto factories, dead power plants, vine-swallowed houses — has a new theme: Olympic venues.

2012-07-15.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-07-15.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-07-15.title: Under Diplomatic Strain, Japan Recalls Envoy in Dispute With China Over Islands
2012-07-15.abstract: Japan temporarily recalled its ambassador to China on Sunday in response to renewed friction over a disputed island group, at a time when it faces discord over sex slaves from World War II.

2012-07-15.title: After Meeting With Clinton, Egypt’s Military Chief Steps Up Political Feud
2012-07-15.abstract: Field Marshal Mohamed Hussein Tantawi challenged the Muslim Brotherhood by saying the army would prevent Egypt from falling to a “certain group,” according to the state news agency.

2012-07-15.title: Wealth Rises in Mongolia, as Does Worry
2012-07-15.abstract: Development of its mining sector has brought new wealth to this remote, landlocked country, but also concerns about corruption and materialism.

2012-07-16.title: U.S. Spends Too Much, Taxes Too Little. Neither Candidate Has Offered a Real Solution
2012-07-16.abstract: The right says Washington spends too much. The left says it taxes too little. Historical and global comparisons suggest both are right, although to different degrees.

2012-07-16.title: After Assassinations, Basque Killers Explain
2012-07-16.abstract: A Spanish government agency has facilitated reconciliation meetings between repentant Basque ETA terrorists and family members of people the ETA has killed.

2012-07-16.title: Failed Graft Prosecution of Former Israeli Premier Spurs Political Questions
2012-07-16.abstract: Ehud Olmert’s allies have asserted that his prosecution was most likely instigated by right-wing political forces in Israel and encouraged by sympathetic American Jews.

2012-07-16.title: North Korea Experts Can See a Lot in a Hemline
2012-07-16.abstract: Analysts are looking at outward signs of a cultural thaw in North Korea, including shorter skirts and high heels, for clues to the thinking of its new leader.

2012-07-16.title: Trying to Eradicate Reports of a Cockroach Invasion
2012-07-16.abstract: Italian news media have reported that red cockroaches, the American type, have invaded Naples, but health and city officials say the stories are not true.

2012-07-17.title: Israel: Gaza Relatives Visit Prisoners
2012-07-17.abstract: Forty people from the Gaza Strip were allowed to visit their husbands and sons inside the Ramon Prison in southern Israel on Monday for the first time in more than five years.

2012-07-17.title: Cuba Hits Wall in 2-Year Push to Expand the Private Sector
2012-07-17.abstract: Nearly two years into an economic overhaul aimed at bolstering private enterprise, new import duties and dogmatic fears are threatening the Cuban government’s stated goals.

2012-07-17.title: Athletes Arrive in London, and Run Into a Dead End
2012-07-17.abstract: Three buses carrying athletes to the Olympic Village lost their way and took four hours instead of 45 minutes.

2012-07-17.title: In Tiny Bean, India’s Dirt-Poor Farmers Strike Gas-Drilling Gold
2012-07-17.abstract: Profits from raising guar, a hard bean that is crucial in the drilling process called hydraulic fracturing, have improved the lives of poor farmers in northwestern India.

2012-07-17.title: Israeli’s Act of Despair Disheartens a Movement
2012-07-17.abstract: Israel’s social justice movement struggled to respond after a protester, Moshe Silman, set himself on fire at one of the largest protests in the country’s history on Saturday.

2012-07-18.title: France: Unesco Awards Prize Financed by Dictator
2012-07-18.abstract: Unesco on Tuesday awarded a prize for research in the life sciences made controversial because it was financed by the dictator of Equatorial Guinea, a tiny, repressive and impoverished state.

2012-07-18.title: As China Talks of Change, Fear Rises on the Risks
2012-07-18.abstract: A small but growing group of children of the Chinese elite is calling for reform as a leadership change looms, but the exact meaning of “reform” is being debated.

2012-07-18.title: China: Teenage Tibetan Monk Dies in Protest’s 44th Self-Immolation
2012-07-18.abstract: A teenage Tibetan monk died Tuesday after setting himself on fire in what has become an increasingly familiar protest against harsh government policies in the region, exile groups said.

2012-07-18.title: Malians Flee as Extremists Tighten Their Grip
2012-07-18.abstract: The vast desert expanse of northern Mali has become a magnet for Islamic extremists, who are imposing a strict form of justice that is prompting tens of thousands of people to flee.

2012-07-18.title: West Bank: Israel Authorizes University in Jewish Settlement
2012-07-18.abstract: An Israeli education committee voted Tuesday to grant full university status to an academic center in Ariel, a large Jewish settlement, making it the first Israeli university in the West Bank.

2012-07-19.title: Fear Stalks Mali’s Refugees Despite Escape to Safety
2012-07-19.abstract: Islamist extremists have prompted more than 90,000 Malian refugees to flee into neighboring Mauritania, where they spoke of the turmoil that pushed them out.

2012-07-19.title: Though Bruised, Netanyahu Is Seen as Secure in Post After Coalition’s Demise
2012-07-19.abstract: Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s image of invincibility has taken a knock, but his political future seems assured because he has no obvious rival for the premiership, Israeli analysts said.

2012-07-19.title: Mapping the Fight in Damascus
2012-07-19.abstract: Government forces have been fighting rebels in parts of the Syrian capital since Sunday.

2012-07-19.title: Mali’s Refugees Escape Turmoil
2012-07-19.abstract: A convoy of refugees escaping Islamic extremists in Mali arrived in Fassala, Mauritania, on Wednesday.

2012-07-19.title: In Spain, a Symbol of Ruin at an Airport to Nowhere
2012-07-19.abstract: The airport, open for a year without receiving a single scheduled flight, is a symbol of the regional spending binge that caused much of Spain’s debt problem.

2012-07-20.title: Paris Wants Smokers to Kick a Bad Habit: Tossing Butts in the Street
2012-07-20.abstract: The city is installing plastic snuffers on public trash bins in an effort to cut down on cigarette waste in gutters and on sidewalks.

2012-07-20.title: Topless Woman Found. Details Sketchy.
2012-07-20.abstract: F.B.I. agents in Miami have arrested two people in connection with the theft of a $3 million painting from a museum in Venezuela, where a fake version hung, unnoticed, for two years.

2012-07-20.title: Obama, on the Trail, Plays for Time on Foreign Policy
2012-07-20.abstract: With the White House in campaign mode nearly 24/7, many of the administration’s biggest foreign policy initiatives have been pushed to the back burner.

2012-07-20.title: As Chaos Grows in Syria, Worries Grow on the Sidelines
2012-07-20.abstract: Many Iranians hailed the Arab Spring last year as a gift from God, but now some are starting to worry about how much might be at stake if the Syrian government collapses.

2012-07-20.title: For Wounded Vets, a Pedal Through Manhattan
2012-07-20.abstract: Nearly 30 injured veterans and active-duty service members pedaled through New York City on Thursday as part of the Wounded Warrior Project’s latest Soldier Ride.

2012-07-21.title: North Korea Said to Remove Military’s Lucrative Export Privilege
2012-07-21.abstract: The move by North Korea’s leader, Kim Jong-un, might be an attempt to rejuvenate the country’s staggering economy and to curtail top generals’ influence.

2012-07-21.title: Inquiry Seeks Accomplices of Bomber in Bulgaria
2012-07-21.abstract: The police are searching for clues to the identity of the bomber who blew up a bus filled with Israeli tourists on Wednesday, focusing new attention on his possible accomplice or accomplices.

2012-07-21.title: Syrians Fleeing Capital Leave Bodies and Bombs Behind
2012-07-21.abstract: One family that managed to escape the deadly conflict and cross into Lebanon described a hellish landscape punctuated by explosions.

2012-07-21.title: In Zimbabwe Land Takeover, a Golden Lining
2012-07-21.abstract: The seizure of white-owned farms was a disaster for Zimbabwe on many levels, but amid that pain, tens of thousands of people got small farm plots and many ended up faring pretty well.

2012-07-21.title: Zimbabwe’s Black Farmers Faring Better After Land Upheavals
2012-07-21.abstract: The seizure of white-owned farms was a disaster for Zimbabwe on many levels, but amid that pain, thousands of people got small farm plots and many found success.

2012-07-22.title: Stuart R. Schram, Nuclear Physicist and Mao Scholar, Dies at 88
2012-07-22.abstract: After working on the Manhattan Project, Mr. Schram published works in French and German and taught himself Russian and Japanese before turning to the study of Mao Zedong.

2012-07-22.title: U.S. Drug War Expands to Africa, a Newer Hub for Cartels
2012-07-22.abstract: The United States, trying to combat Latin American groups that are using Africa to smuggle cocaine into Europe, has begun training an elite counternarcotics police unit in Ghana.

2012-07-22.title: A Peeping Tom and Toxic Pigs: China’s Olympic Headaches
2012-07-22.abstract: Elite Chinese athletes in their final preparations for the Olympics, which open on Friday, are now focusing on special training routines while eliminating distractions. One distraction that the women swimmers didn’t need was a peeping Tom in their locker room — along with the politics over a visit by the Dalai Lama and Chinese pork dangerously loaded with steroids.

2012-07-22.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-07-22.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-07-22.title: Both Sides Claim Progress as Violence Continues in Syria
2012-07-22.abstract: Violent clashes continued on Sunday in Aleppo and Damascus as Syrian government forces fought to regain control over areas that rebels claimed.

2012-07-23.title: Mapping Toilets in a Mumbai Slum Yields Unexpected Results
2012-07-23.abstract: Cheetah Camp has one working toilet per 170 people, Harvard students found.

2012-07-23.title: Reports of Forced Abortions Fuel Push to End Chinese Law
2012-07-23.abstract: Accounts of women being coerced into late-term abortions by local officials have thrust a policy that penalizes families in China for having more than one child into the spotlight.

2012-07-23.title: 3 Face Negligence Charges in Reaction to Russia Flood
2012-07-23.abstract: The mayor, district leader and local emergency services chief in Krymsk were arrested and accused of failing to warn the public before a 20-foot wall of water hit the city this month.

2012-07-23.title: Halfway Through the Season, an F1 Series That Has Never Failed to Thrill
2012-07-23.abstract: In Germany, Fernando Alonso becomes the only driver to win three races this season, the longest in Formula One history.

2012-07-23.title: Top Afghans Tied to ’90s Carnage, Researchers Say
2012-07-23.abstract: The powerful men accused of responsibility, directly or indirectly, for tens of thousands of deaths are named in the pages of a monumental 800-page report.

2012-07-24.title: An Inside Look at China’s Most Famous Political Prisoner
2012-07-24.abstract: A friend of the Nobel laureate Liu Xiaobo has just published a new biography of Mr. Liu, China’s most famous political prisoner. Yu Jie said he has tried to cast Mr. Liu as a profound and important thinker, as well as a flawed human being. He also has some sharp and dismissive words for the dissident artist Ai Weiwei.

2012-07-24.title: Afghan Cabinet Raises Concern About Mining Legislation, to West’s Unease
2012-07-24.abstract: The cabinet’s rejection on Wednesday of draft legislation that would, among other things, provide foreign investors with more certainty caught Western diplomats in Kabul off guard.

2012-07-24.title: Israel Seeks Army Use of West Bank Area
2012-07-24.abstract: Palestinian advocates say the request to the Supreme Court is part of an undeclared policy of evictions, which the government denies.

2012-07-24.title: Contrary to Gossip, Pyramids Have No Date With the Wrecking Ball
2012-07-24.abstract: The spread of unfounded stories about an Islamist clamor to destroy Egypt’s monuments shows how a rumor can take on a life of its own in this tumultuous period.

2012-07-24.title: Inquiry Sees Chaos in Evacuations After Japan Tsunami
2012-07-24.abstract: Children were left in areas with high radiation levels as computer-aided predictions on radioactive releases went unheeded, a government-sponsored inquiry reported Monday.

2012-07-25.title: Foreign Policy Experts Discuss Ways to Avert Future Genocide
2012-07-25.abstract: With the specter of mounting violence in some countries, the threat of future mass killings was a prime concern at a symposium in Washington.

2012-07-25.title: Police Official Went to Fight for Taliban, Afghans Say
2012-07-25.abstract: The move came as an American engineer was said to have been killed near Kabul.

2012-07-25.title: With Slow Growth, Jobs Elusive for Many in South Africa, Report Says
2012-07-25.abstract: The report indicated that economic disparities among citizens and failing schools in some areas have limited social mobility for many of the nation’s children.

2012-07-25.title: Al Qaeda Taking Deadly New Role in Syria’s Conflict
2012-07-25.abstract: The terrorist network has helped to alter the Syrian revolution, injecting the weapon it perfected in Iraq — suicide bombings — into the battle against President Bashar al-Assad.

2012-07-25.title: General Confirms Defection From Syria
2012-07-25.abstract: Brig. Gen. Manaf Tlass, the former commander of an elite Republican Guard unit in Syria, made an announcement on television.

2012-07-26.title: Egypt: Mix of Old and New in Cabinet
2012-07-26.abstract: Egypt’s next government will include representation of different segments of society, the official Middle East News Agency said.

2012-07-26.title: Gaza: Fears of Economic Isolation
2012-07-26.abstract: The United Nations’ top Middle East diplomat expressed concern on Wednesday about the precarious finances of the Palestinian Authority and the economic isolation of Gaza.

2012-07-26.title: Iran: New Currency Restrictions
2012-07-26.abstract: Apparently worried about its foreign cash supply because of the West’s economic sanctions, Iran announced new currency restrictions.

2012-07-26.title: Belarus: Leader Barred From Olympics
2012-07-26.abstract: Britain said it would not waive a visa restriction for Belarus’s autocratic president, Aleksandr G. Lukashenko, and others accused of human rights abuses.

2012-07-26.title: Political Worries in U.S. and China Color Obama Aide’s Beijing Visit
2012-07-26.abstract: Thomas E. Donilon, the national security adviser, met with Chinese leaders in Beijing who are uneasy about their country’s role as a punching bag in the American presidential race.

2012-07-27.title: An Epic Downpour Wipes Away a Capital’s Sheen
2012-07-27.abstract: A rainstorm, the heaviest to hit Beijing in decades, has prompted tough questions about the management of the city after flooding left at least 77 people dead.

2012-07-27.title: Grim Village Shaken by Gruesome Mystery
2012-07-27.abstract: The discovery of 248 fetuses in a wooded area of a Siberian village is the area’s latest representation of death and abandonment.

2012-07-27.title: Quiet Duo Forged Road Deal for U.S. and Pakistan
2012-07-27.abstract: A political deal broke a seven-month deadlock that had stranded more than 11,000 military shipping containers bound for Afghanistan.

2012-07-27.title: Ahead of Olympic Games, a Sudanese Athlete Seeks Political Asylum in London
2012-07-27.abstract: The athlete, a runner reportedly in his 20s, follows a long tradition of defections at international sports competitions.

2012-07-27.title: Rise Is Seen in Cyberattacks Targeting U.S. Infrastructure
2012-07-27.abstract: Gen. Keith B. Alexander of the National Security Agency said that the nation was not fully prepared for the attacks on electricity grids, water supplies and other targets.

2012-07-28.title: A Champion for the Displaced in Israel
2012-07-28.abstract: The lawyer Michael Sfard has brought scores of human rights and land-use cases challenging Israel’s occupation of the Palestinian territories it acquired in 1967, and represented hundreds of soldiers refusing to serve.

2012-07-28.title: In Conservative Japan Enclave, Antinuclear Candidate Gains Ground
2012-07-28.abstract: Nearly a year and a half after Japan’s nuclear disaster, the election has evolved into an informal referendum on nuclear power’s place in the country’s future.

2012-07-28.title: Zimbabwe: Food Aid Needed
2012-07-28.abstract: Erratic rains and farming problems have reduced this year’s maize harvest in Zimbabwe by a third, and 1.6 million people will need food aid, the World Food Program said Friday.

2012-07-28.title: Sudan: Asylum Report Denied
2012-07-28.abstract: The Sudanese Embassy in Britain denied reports on Friday that one of its Olympic athletes had sought asylum in Britain.

2012-07-28.title: France Is Expected to Pass a New Harassment Law
2012-07-28.abstract: A far-reaching sexual harassment law, considered a significant step toward women’s full rights in France, is expected to be approved by lawmakers next week.

2012-07-29.title: Taking It to the Street in China
2012-07-29.abstract: Large public protests are coming faster and bloodier now in China, with two riots just this month over dangerous and ill-conceived factory projects. With citizens’ outrage ever more evident, both online and off, perhaps we’re seeing “the Chinese street” — as a corollary of the Arab street — beginning to emerge.

2012-07-29.title: Newswallah: Long Reads Edition
2012-07-29.abstract: Top magazine and long-form articles this week.

2012-07-29.title: Syrian Says Foreign Plot Is to Blame for Violence
2012-07-29.abstract: The foreign minister’s comments reflected the pressures on the Syrian government as it fights an emboldened insurgency on several fronts, most crucially in Aleppo.

2012-07-29.title: Thousands Protest China’s Plans for Hong Kong Schools
2012-07-29.abstract: Hong Kong residents took to the streets a day after the city’s education minister warned that such demonstrations would not stop or delay the process.

2012-07-29.title: Syrian Refugees Are Stung by a Hostile Reception in Iraq
2012-07-29.abstract: Alone among Syria’s Muslim neighbors, Iraq is actively resisting receiving refugees from the conflict across the border, and is making those who do arrive anything but comfortable.

2012-07-30.title: West Bank’s Emerging Silicon Valley Evades Issues of Borders
2012-07-30.abstract: Many say the small but burgeoning Palestinian tech sector is on the verge of big things, aided by the lack of impediments to movement that Israel imposes in the name of security.

2012-07-30.title: Allies Rebuke Pakistan on Cross-Border Attacks
2012-07-30.abstract: The American-led coalition bluntly rebutted a Pakistani official’s statement that allied forces had done little over all to stop Pakistan Taliban militants from attacking Pakistani forces from Afghanistan.

2012-07-30.title: Nuclear Energy Critic Loses Japan Election
2012-07-30.abstract: The loss underscored the challenge that those opposed to nuclear energy face in translating recent mass protests into votes.

2012-07-30.title: South America Sees Drug Path to Legalization
2012-07-30.abstract: Leaders are considering more permissive policies, such as the legalization and regulation of drug use and even drug transport, as a way to fight the spread of drug-related violence.

2012-07-30.title: Para frenar tráfico de marihuana, Uruguay considera adueñarse del negocio
2012-07-30.abstract: En América Latina, los lideres consideran políticas mas permisivas como una manera de luchar contra la proliferación de violencia relacionada al tráfico drogas.

2012-07-31.title: Namibia: Court Says Government Coerced Women Into Sterilization
2012-07-31.abstract: The High Court ruled Monday that the government had coerced three women infected with H.I.V. to agree to be sterilized after giving birth, the Southern Africa Litigation Center said.

2012-07-31.title: New Boldness From Militants Poses Risk to U.S.-Pakistan Ties
2012-07-31.abstract: The Haqqanis’ pursuit of mass American casualties, combined with simmering political tension, has reduced room for ambiguity between Pakistan and the United States.

2012-07-31.title: Escaping Syria to a Barren Plain of Sweat and Grit
2012-07-31.abstract: The Ceylanpinar camp, home to more than 12,000 Syrians who fled their country’s civil war, is in the Turkish equivalent of Siberia, a dusty, rocky plain in southeastern Turkey.

2012-07-31.title: Syrians Find Hard Shelter in Turkey
2012-07-31.abstract: For Syrians escaping the civil war in their country, the large camp in Ceylanpinar, Turkey, is a hard place to rest comfortably.

2012-07-31.title: U.S. and Pakistan: Allies on the Brink
2012-07-31.abstract: Declan Walsh, the New York Times bureau chief in Pakistan, explores the relationship between the United States and Pakistan and the countries’ differing strategic interests.
